name: Sliding Window with Last-Seen Map
summary: Grow a window to the right one character at a time; when a repeat inside the window appears, jump the left edge just past the previous occurrence.
visualizationIntent: init: show the whole string with an empty window at the left. loop: the right edge extends to include the next character, which flashes as current, and the highlighted window band grows. move: on a repeat, the left edge leaps to just after the earlier copy, collapsing the window and marking the skipped-over cells as visited. update: whenever the window is the longest so far, record its length as the new best. return: report the largest window length seen.

lineExplanations.3: Create a map remembering the most recent index where each character was seen.
lineExplanations.4: Start the left edge of the window at the beginning of the string.
lineExplanations.5: Track the length of the longest valid window found so far.
lineExplanations.6: Extend the right edge across every character of the string.
lineExplanations.7: Read the character now entering the window on the right.
lineExplanations.8: Check whether this character already sits inside the current window.
lineExplanations.9: Jump the left edge to just after the earlier copy so the window has no repeats.
lineExplanations.10: Record this character's latest index in the map.
lineExplanations.12: Check whether this window is the longest seen so far.
lineExplanations.13: Update the best length to this new, longer window.
lineExplanations.14: Return the length of the longest substring without repeats.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper expected by the platform.
syntaxExplanations.2: def lengthOfLongestSubstring(self, s): defines the method receiving the string s.
syntaxExplanations.3: seen = {} creates an empty dictionary of character to last index.
syntaxExplanations.4: left = 0 sets the left window edge to the first index.
syntaxExplanations.5: best = 0 initializes the largest window length found.
syntaxExplanations.6: for right in range(len(s)): loops right over every index of s.
syntaxExplanations.7: ch = s[right] reads the character at the right edge.
syntaxExplanations.8: if ch in seen and seen[ch] >= left: is true when ch already lies within the window.
syntaxExplanations.9: left = seen[ch] + 1 moves the left edge past the earlier duplicate.
syntaxExplanations.10: seen[ch] = right stores the current index as this character's latest position.
syntaxExplanations.11: window = right - left + 1 computes the number of characters in the window.
syntaxExplanations.12: if window > best: checks whether this window beats the record.
syntaxExplanations.13: best = window stores the new record length.
syntaxExplanations.14: return best sends back the answer.
